questions.question: Explain the three rules that define the happened-before relation in a distributed system.
expectedPoints: Rule 1: If two events occur in the same process, the earlier event happens before the later one.
Rule 2: A send event of a message happens before the corresponding receive event of that message.
Rule 3: The relation is transitive; if A happens before B and B happens before C, then A happens before C.

questions.question: Describe the problem of capturing a global state in a distributed system. What is an inconsistent cut and how can it occur?
expectedPoints: A global state is the collection of all process states and channel states.
The challenge is that processes and channels are independent, making simultaneous state collection impossible.
An inconsistent cut (state) occurs if it includes the effect of a message (receive recorded) but not its cause (send not recorded).
This state is considered 'inconsistent' because it violates causality and represents a situation that could never have happened in a real execution.
It occurs when the state of the receiver is collected after the message is received, but the state of the sender is collected before the message is sent.
